reference_letter:
Date: 14 February 2026

Dear GP,

Re: Patient 1, DOB 03/03/1958, NHS No. 328 242 2679

Summary

Patient 1, a 68-year-old woman with type 2 diabetes mellitus and hypertension, attended for diabetes review. Her HbA1c has risen to 6.8 mmol/mol. Metformin 1g twice daily will continue with adherence reinforcement and repeat blood tests in three months.

History of presenting complaint

Patient 1 attended for routine review. She reported rising blood glucose readings and afternoon fatigue affecting her daily routine over the past few weeks.

Past medical history

Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus, Hypertension.

Current medications

Metformin 1g twice daily (unchanged)

Examination findings

No examination findings were documented during this consultation.

Investigation results

HbA1c was 6.8 mmol/mol on 06/01/2026, rising from 3.2 mmol/mol on 19/12/2025. This confirms deterioration in glycaemic control.

Assessment

Patient 1 has suboptimal glycaemic control with a rising HbA1c.

Plan

1. Continue metformin 1g twice daily with adherence reinforcement.
2. Lifestyle measures including diet, activity, and sleep were discussed.
3. Repeat blood tests in three months.

Advice to patient

1. Safety-net advice and warning symptoms reviewed.
2. Advised to seek urgent care if red-flag symptoms worsen.

Warm regards,

Dr Sarah Chen
Consultant

Date: 14 February 2026

Dear GP,

Re: Patient 2, DOB 22/02/1996, NHS No. 716 127 4257

Summary

Patient 2, a 30-year-old woman with stable angina and hypertension, attended for cardiology follow-up. She reported intermittent exertional chest discomfort. Troponin was 13.0 ng/L. Current medications will continue, with a home blood pressure diary and follow-up in eight weeks.

History of presenting complaint

Patient 2 attended for follow-up review. She reported intermittent exertional chest discomfort affecting her daily routine over the past few weeks.

Past medical history

Stable Angina, Hypertension.

Current medications

Atorvastatin 40 mg once nightly (unchanged)

Examination findings

No examination findings were documented during this consultation.

Investigation results

Troponin was 13.0 ng/L on 06/01/2026, rising from 8.7 ng/L on 07/11/2025.

Assessment

Patient 2 has stable angina with rising troponin levels.

Plan

1. Continue atorvastatin 40 mg nightly with adherence reinforcement.
2. Lifestyle measures including diet, activity, and sleep discussed.
3. Home blood pressure diary requested.
4. Cardiology follow-up in eight weeks.

Advice to patient

1. Advised to seek urgent care if symptoms worsen.

Warm regards,

Dr Sarah Chen
Consultant

Date: 14 February 2026

Dear GP,

Re: Patient 3, DOB 28/02/1970, NHS No. 925 263 7924

Summary

Patient 3, a 56-year-old man with moderate persistent asthma and hypertension, attended for respiratory review. He reported frequent reliever inhaler use and night symptoms. Peak flow was 77.8 L/min. An inhaler technique check and follow-up in six weeks were arranged.

History of presenting complaint

Patient 3 attended for asthma review. He reported frequent reliever inhaler use and night symptoms affecting his daily routine over the past few weeks.

Past medical history

Moderate Persistent Asthma, Hypertension.

Current medications

Budesonide/Formoterol 200/6 mcg twice daily (unchanged)

Examination findings

No examination findings were documented during this consultation.

Investigation results

Peak flow was 77.8 L/min on 23/01/2026, rising from 69.9 L/min on 11/12/2025.

Assessment

Patient 3 has poorly controlled asthma with frequent reliever use and night symptoms.

Plan

1. Continue Budesonide/Formoterol 200/6 mcg twice daily with adherence reinforcement.
2. Inhaler technique check arranged.
3. Follow-up in six weeks.

Advice to patient

1. Advised to seek urgent care if symptoms worsen.

Warm regards,

Dr Sarah Chen
Consultant

Date: 14 February 2026

Dear GP,

Re: Patient 4, DOB 05/03/1950, NHS No. 467 967 6635

Summary

Patient 4, a 76-year-old man with heart failure with reduced ejection fraction and hypertension, attended for heart failure review. He reported breathlessness on exertion and ankle swelling. BNP was 13.8 pg/mL. Daily weight monitoring and heart failure nurse review in four weeks were arranged.

History of presenting complaint

Patient 4 attended for heart failure review. He reported breathlessness on exertion and ankle swelling affecting his daily routine over the past few weeks.

Past medical history

Heart Failure with Reduced Ejection Fraction, Hypertension.

Current medications

Furosemide 40 mg once daily (unchanged)

Examination findings

No examination findings were documented during this consultation.

Investigation results

BNP was 13.8 pg/mL on 24/01/2026, rising from 11.9 pg/mL on 25/12/2025.

Assessment

Patient 4 has heart failure with worsening breathlessness on exertion and ankle swelling. BNP is rising.

Plan

1. Continue furosemide 40 mg once daily with adherence reinforcement.
2. Daily weight monitoring advised.
3. Lifestyle measures including fluid and salt intake discussed.
4. Heart failure nurse review in four weeks.

Advice to patient

1. Advised to seek urgent care if symptoms worsen.

Warm regards,

Dr Sarah Chen
Consultant

Date: 14 February 2026

Dear GP,

Re: Patient 5, DOB 01/03/1964, NHS No. 665 400 6925

Summary

Patient 5, a 62-year-old woman with major depressive disorder and hypertension, attended for mental health review. She reported low mood, poor sleep, and reduced motivation. PHQ-9 was 92.6. Psychological support will continue with reassessment in four weeks.

History of presenting complaint

Patient 5 attended for review. She reported low mood, poor sleep, and reduced motivation affecting her daily routine over the past few weeks.

Past medical history

Major Depressive Disorder, Hypertension.

Current medications

Sertraline 100 mg once daily (unchanged)

Examination findings

No examination findings were documented during this consultation.

Investigation results

PHQ-9 score was 92.6 on 28/01/2026, rising from 90.7 on 22/12/2025.

Assessment

Patient 5 has major depressive disorder with persistent low mood, poor sleep, and reduced motivation despite sertraline 100 mg.

Plan

1. Continue sertraline 100 mg once daily with adherence reinforcement.
2. Psychological support will continue.
3. Reassessment in four weeks.

Advice to patient

1. Lifestyle measures including sleep, diet, and activity discussed.
2. Advised to seek urgent care if symptoms worsen significantly.

Warm regards,

Dr Sarah Chen
Consultant
